zh.Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgium: 比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文
zh.Affligem, Brabant, Lotharingia, Holy Rom: 比利時布拉班特阿福利格姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida van Leuven-Brabant於1108年出生在比利時布拉班特洛文時，她的父親Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen48歲，母親Ida de Chiny31歲。她和Arnold I. von Kleve至少有1個兒子。她去世於1162年7月27日在法國皮卡迪索姆享年54歲，埋葬在荷蘭德倫特可盧斯特。

zh-hans.Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgium: 比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文
zh-hans.Affligem, Brabant, Lotharingia, Holy Rom: 比利时布拉班特阿福利格姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ida van Leuven-Brabant于1108年出生在比利时布拉班特洛文时，她的父亲Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen48岁，母亲Ida de Chiny31岁。她和Arnold I. von Kleve至少有1个儿子。她去世于1162年7月27日在法国皮卡迪索姆享年54岁，埋葬在荷兰德倫特可盧斯特。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Ida van Leuven-Brabant was born in 1108, in Leuven, Brabant, Belgium, her father, Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, was 48 and her mother, Ida de Chiny, was 31. She had at least 1 son with Arnold I. von Kleve. She died on 27 July 1162, in Somme, Picardie, France, at the age of 54, and was buried in Klooster, Drenthe, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ida van Leuven-Brabant est née en 1108, à Leuven, Brabant, Belgique, son père, Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Ida de Chiny, avait 31 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Arnold I. von Kleve. Elle est décédée le 27 juillet 1162, à Somme, Picardie, France, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Klooster, Drenthe, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ida van Leuven-Brabant 1108 in Leuven, Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen 48 und ihre Mutter Ida de Chiny 31. Sie hatte mit Arnold I. von Kleve mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 27. Juli 1162 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich und wurde in Klooster, Drenthe, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ida van Leuven-Brabant è nata il nel 1108 nel Leuven, Brabante, Belgio, sua padre, Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Ida de Chiny, aveva 31 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Arnold I. von Kleve. Morì il 27 luglio 1162, nel Somme, Picardie, Francia, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Klooster, Drenthe, Paesi Bassi.

ja.Klooster, Drenthe, Pays-Bas: オランダドレンスクロオスター
ja.Leuven, Brabant, Belgium: ベルギーブラバントレウベン
ja.Somme, Picardie, France: フランスピカーディソンメ
ja.Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgium: ベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベン
ja.Affligem, Brabant, Lotharingia, Holy Rom: ベルギーブラバントアフリジェム
ja.lifeSummary: Ida van Leuven-Brabantが1108年にベルギーブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingenは48歳，母Ida de Chinyは31歳であった。彼女はArnold I. von Kleveとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1162年7月27日にフランスピカーディソンメで54歳で死去し，オランダドレンスクロオスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ida van Leuven-Brabant(이)가 1108년 벨기에 브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ida de Chiny(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Arnold I. von Kleve(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1162년 7월 27일 프랑스 피카디 솜에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 드렌테 클루스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ida van Leuven-Brabant nasceu em 1108, em Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, seu pai, Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Ida de Chiny, tinha 31 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Arnold I. von Kleve. Ela faleceu em 27 de julho de 1162, em Somme, Picardie, França, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Klooster, Drente, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida van Leuven-Brabant родилась в 1108 г. в местности леувен, брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Ida de Chiny, 31. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Arnold I. von Kleve. Она умерла 27 июля 1162 г. в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности клустир, дрент, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ida van Leuven-Brabant nació en 1108, en Leuven, Brabante, Bélgica, su papá, Godfried I van Leuven-Brabant hertog van Neder-Lotharingen, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Ida de Chiny, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Arnold I. von Kleve. Murió el 27 de julio de 1162, en Somme, Picardie, Francia, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultada en Klooster, Drente, Países Bajos.
